intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard feels more approachable and less clinical when interface elements use gentle curves rather than sharp edges
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, friendly, non-intimidating aesthetics for a personal health tool
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, modern, or structured visual language—possibly more minimalist or geometric

evidence.hypothesis: A health dashboard feels more approachable and less clinical when interface elements use gentle curves rather than sharp edges
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward warm, friendly, non-intimidating aesthetics for a personal health tool

facade.hypothesis: Users building a health dashboard prefer warm, approachable geometry over clinical sharpness
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design direction leans toward organic, friendly, low-stress visual language—think wellness app rather than fitness tracker
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cleaner, more structured geometry—either minimal/stark OR complex/technical. Signals openness to cooler or more clinical mood.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer lightweight periodic snapshots over continuous dense dashboards
facade.axisTargeted: Granularity & frequency: real-time metrics vs. periodic summaries
facade.label: Weekly summary cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values temporal digests, compression, and reflection over always-on data. May prefer narrative synthesis over raw numbers.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants live data, continuous monitoring, or drill-down detail on demand. Dashboards should be always-visible, not weekly review tools.

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left navigation paired with scrollable card layouts will feel organized without overwhelming for health experiment tracking
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values stable reference frame + prefers visual chunking of data over flat lists
facade.rejectImplies: User wants immersive full-width focus or tab-based switching + finds sidebars claustrophobic or distracting

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer panels that glide in with easing over instant pop-ups, especially when reviewing historical experiment data
facade.axisTargeted: fluid transitions vs snappy instant appearance
facade.label: Smooth slide-in panels
facade.acceptImplies: User values graceful motion for data reveals; likely prefers animated state changes across dashboard (loading states, metric updates, drill-downs)
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes speed and clarity over motion; dashboard should use instant visibility, minimal animation, faster cognitive processing of health metrics

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, lightweight tone over clinical language when logging health data
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality: friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Use warm, conversational microcopy ('How's your energy today?' vs 'Log vitality metric'). Favor approachable language throughout the interface.
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward more formal, clinical tone. Use precise medical/scientific terminology. Adopt professional, detached voice in all copy.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard using dark mode with high contrast text feels less visually fatiguing during frequent check-ins and creates a sense of calm focus rather than clinical brightness
facade.label: Dark background, bright text
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers reduced visual intensity and may favor evening-friendly or low-light viewing modes for personal health tracking
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers bright, airy interfaces that feel open and optimistic—light backgrounds align better with wellness and clarity messaging

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
